children.value: Every panel on this page runs

. Cells with a play button are fully editable: change the numbers, press play (or Ctrl-Enter), and the result updates. Come back here whenever you need to crunch a quick number, make a plot, take a derivative, or look at an orbital, without leaving the website.
Already imported for you on this page:
. The button at the top opens the whole page as an editable notebook in molab.

model.html: import%20marimo%20as%20mo%0Aimport%20numpy%20as%20np%0Aimport%20matplotlib.pyplot%20as%20plt%0Aimport%20plotly.graph_objects%20as%20go%0Aimport%20scipy.constants%20as%20const%0Aimport%20sympy%20as%20sp%0Afrom%20scipy.special%20import%20sph_harm_y%2C%20genlaguerre%2C%20factorial%0Afrom%20matplotlib.colors%20import%20to_hex
model.molabNotebookCode:
# /// script # requires-python = ">=3.10" # dependencies = [ # "numpy", # "scipy", # "matplotlib", # "sympy", # "plotly", # ] # /// import marimo __generated_with = "0.23.14" app = marimo.App() @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" # Python Calculator :::{note} **Your quantum chemistry workbench** Every panel on this page runs **live in your browser**. Cells with a play button are fully editable: change the numbers, press play (or Ctrl-Enter), and the result updates. Come back here whenever you need to crunch a quick number, make a plot, take a derivative, or look at an orbital, without leaving the website. Already imported for you on this page: `numpy as np`, `matplotlib.pyplot as plt`, `sympy as sp`, and `scipy.constants as const`. The button at the top opens the whole page as an editable notebook in molab. ::: """) return @app.cell def _(): import marimo as mo import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import plotly.graph_objects as go import scipy.constants as const import sympy as sp from scipy.special import sph_harm_y, genlaguerre, factorial from matplotlib.colors import to_hex return ( const, factorial, genlaguerre, go, mo, np, plt, sp, sph_harm_y, to_hex, ) @app.function def wavelength_to_rgb(nm): if 380 <= nm < 440: rgb = (-(nm - 440) / 60, 0.0, 1.0) elif 440 <= nm < 490: rgb = (0.0, (nm - 440) / 50, 1.0) elif 490 <= nm < 510: rgb = (0.0, 1.0, -(nm - 510) / 20) elif 510 <= nm < 580: rgb = ((nm - 510) / 70, 1.0, 0.0) elif 580 <= nm < 645: rgb = (1.0, -(nm - 645) / 65, 0.0) elif 645 <= nm <= 750: rgb = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0) else: rgb = (0.55, 0.55, 0.55) return rgb @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" ### 1. Constants and quick calculations `scipy.constants` knows every physical constant ([full list](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/constants.html)): """) return @app.cell def _(const, mo): mo.md(f""" | constant | symbol | value | |---|---|---| | Planck | $h$ | {const.h:.4e} J s | | speed of light | $c$ | {const.c:.4e} m/s | | electron charge | $e$ | {const.e:.4e} C | | electron mass | $m_e$ | {const.m_e:.4e} kg | | Boltzmann | $k_B$ | {const.k:.4e} J/K | | Avogadro | $N_A$ | {const.N_A:.4e} 1/mol | | Bohr radius | $a_0$ | {const.value("Bohr radius"):.4e} m | | Rydberg | $R_\\infty$ | {const.Rydberg:.4e} 1/m | """) return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" A scratchpad, seeded with a photon-energy calculation. Edit it and press play; the value of the last line is displayed: """) return @app.cell def _(const): lam = 532e-9 # a green laser pointer E_photon = const.h * const.c / lam E_photon / const.e # in electron volts return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" Or let the slider do the arithmetic. Can this photon break a C-C bond? """) return @app.cell def _(mo): lam_q = mo.ui.slider(start=200, stop=1000, step=2, value=532, show_value=True, label="photon wavelength (nm)") lam_q return (lam_q,) @app.cell def _(const, lam_q, mo, to_hex): e_q = const.h * const.c / (lam_q.value * 1e-9) kjmol_q = e_q * const.N_A / 1000 region_q = "ultraviolet" if lam_q.value < 380 else ("visible" if lam_q.value <= 750 else "infrared") verdict_q = "YES, breaks a C-C bond (347 kJ/mol)" if kjmol_q > 347 else "no, too weak for a C-C bond (347 kJ/mol)" mo.vstack([ mo.md(f"A **{lam_q.value} nm** photon ({region_q}) carries **{e_q/const.e:.2f} eV** " f"= **{kjmol_q:.0f} kJ/mol**: **{verdict_q}**"), mo.Html(f"
"), ]) return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" ### 2. Unit converter """) return @app.cell def _(mo): to_joules = { "J": 1.0, "eV": 1.602176634e-19, "cm^-1": 1.986445857e-23, "kJ/mol": 1000 / 6.02214076e23, "hartree": 4.3597447222e-18, "Hz": 6.62607015e-34, } amount_c = mo.ui.number(value=1.0, step=0.1, label="value") unit_c = mo.ui.dropdown(options=list(to_joules), value="eV", label="from unit") mo.hstack([amount_c, unit_c], justify="start", gap=1.5) return amount_c, to_joules, unit_c @app.cell def _(amount_c, mo, to_joules, unit_c): val_c = amount_c.value if amount_c.value is not None else 0.0 joules_c = val_c * to_joules[unit_c.value] rows_c = "\n".join(f"| {u} | {joules_c / f:.6g} |" for u, f in to_joules.items()) mo.md(f"**{val_c:g} {unit_c.value}** equals:\n\n| unit | value |\n|:--|--:|\n{rows_c}") return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" ### 3. Plotting panel A ready-to-edit plotting template. Swap in any function of `x_p`; the slider is yours to repurpose: """) return @app.cell def _(mo): k_p = mo.ui.slider(1, 12, step=1, value=3, show_value=True, label="parameter k") k_p return (k_p,) @app.cell def _(k_p, np, plt): x_p = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 500) y_p = np.sin(k_p.value * x_p) * np.exp(-x_p / 4) # edit me fig_p, ax_p = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3.5)) ax_p.plot(x_p, y_p, lw=2) ax_p.set_xlabel("x") ax_p.grid(True, ls="--", alpha=0.4) fig_p return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" ### 4. Symbolic window Derivatives, integrals, and equation solving with `sympy`; results render as typeset math. Each pad is editable: """) return @app.cell def _(sp): u = sp.symbols("u") sp.diff(sp.exp(-u**2) * sp.sin(u), u) # derivative pad return @app.cell def _(sp): w = sp.symbols("w", positive=True) sp.integrate(w**2 * sp.exp(-w), (w, 0, sp.oo)) # integral pad return @app.cell def _(sp): q = sp.symbols("q") sp.solve(q**3 - 6*q**2 + 11*q - 6, q) # solver pad return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" ### 5. Orbital visualizer Pick quantum numbers; the menus only ever offer valid combinations. Drag to rotate the orbital (blue and red are the wavefunction's positive and negative lobes): """) return @app.cell def _(factorial, genlaguerre, np): def radial_c(r, n=1, l=0): pre = np.sqrt(((2 / n) ** 3 * factorial(n - l - 1)) / (2 * n * factorial(n + l))) p = 2 * r / n return pre * np.exp(-p / 2) * p**l * genlaguerre(n - l - 1, 2 * l + 1)(p) return (radial_c,) @app.cell def _(mo): n_v = mo.ui.slider(1, 5, step=1, value=2, show_value=True, label="n") n_v return (n_v,) @app.cell def _(mo, n_v): l_v = mo.ui.dropdown(options={str(v): v for v in range(n_v.value)}, value=str(n_v.value - 1), label="l") l_v return (l_v,) @app.cell def _(l_v, mo): m_v = mo.ui.dropdown(options={str(v): v for v in range(-l_v.value, l_v.value + 1)}, value="0", label="m") m_v return (m_v,) @app.cell def _(go, l_v, m_v, n_v, np, radial_c, sph_harm_y): ext_v = 6.0 * n_v.value g_v = np.linspace(-ext_v, ext_v, 45) X_v, Y_v, Z_v = np.meshgrid(g_v, g_v, g_v, indexing="ij") r_v = np.sqrt(X_v**2 + Y_v**2 + Z_v**2) th_v = np.where(np.isclose(r_v, 0.0), 0.0, np.arccos(Z_v / (r_v + 1e-12))) ph_v = np.arctan2(Y_v + 1e-10, X_v) psi_v = (radial_c(r_v, n_v.value, l_v.value) * sph_harm_y(l_v.value, m_v.value, th_v, ph_v)).real amp_v = 0.5 * np.abs(psi_v).max() fig_v = go.Figure(data=go.Isosurface( x=X_v.flatten(), y=Y_v.flatten(), z=Z_v.flatten(), value=psi_v.flatten(), colorscale="RdBu", isomin=-amp_v, isomax=amp_v, surface_count=2, showscale=False, caps=dict(x_show=False, y_show=False, z_show=False), )) fig_v.update_layout(scene=dict(aspectmode="data"), width=660, height=460, title_text=f"orbital ({n_v.value}, {l_v.value}, {m_v.value})") fig_v return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" ### 6. Orbital overlap Bonding starts here: slide two 1s orbitals together and watch their **overlap integral** $S(d) = e^{-d}\left(1 + d + d^2/3\right)$ grow (distances in Bohr radii). The shaded region is the product $\psi_A \psi_B$ whose integral is $S$: """) return @app.cell def _(mo): d_sep = mo.ui.slider(0.5, 8.0, step=0.25, value=2.0, show_value=True, label="internuclear distance d (Bohr)") d_sep return (d_sep,) @app.cell def _(d_sep, np, plt): d_val = d_sep.value x_o = np.linspace(-8, 8, 800) psi_A = np.exp(-np.abs(x_o + d_val / 2)) / np.sqrt(np.pi) psi_B = np.exp(-np.abs(x_o - d_val / 2)) / np.sqrt(np.pi) S_curve_d = np.linspace(0.01, 8, 200) S_curve = np.exp(-S_curve_d) * (1 + S_curve_d + S_curve_d**2 / 3) S_now = np.exp(-d_val) * (1 + d_val + d_val**2 / 3) fig_o, (ax_oa, ax_ob) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9.5, 3.6)) ax_oa.plot(x_o, psi_A, lw=1.8, color="steelblue", label=r"$\psi_A$ (1s)") ax_oa.plot(x_o, psi_B, lw=1.8, color="seagreen", label=r"$\psi_B$ (1s)") ax_oa.fill_between(x_o, psi_A * psi_B * 6, color="crimson", alpha=0.35, label=r"$\psi_A \psi_B$ (scaled)") ax_oa.axvline(-d_val / 2, color="0.6", ls=":", lw=1) ax_oa.axvline(d_val / 2, color="0.6", ls=":", lw=1) ax_oa.set_xlabel("x (Bohr)") ax_oa.legend(fontsize=8, frameon=False) ax_oa.set_title("two 1s orbitals (cut along the bond axis)", fontsize=10) ax_ob.plot(S_curve_d, S_curve, lw=2, color="0.4") ax_ob.plot(d_val, S_now, "o", ms=9, color="crimson") ax_ob.set_xlabel("separation d (Bohr)") ax_ob.set_ylabel("overlap S(d)") ax_ob.set_title(f"S({d_val:.2f}) = {S_now:.3f}", fontsize=10) ax_ob.set_ylim(0, 1.02) fig_o.tight_layout() fig_o return @app.cell def _(mo): mo.md(""" At $d = 0$ the orbitals coincide and $S = 1$; by $d \approx 8\,a_0$ the overlap is essentially gone. The window where $S$ is a few tenths is exactly where [chemical bonds live](../ch08/02-hydrogen-molecule-ion.md). :::{tip} Want more room? The molab button at the top opens this whole page as an editable notebook where you can add as many cells as you like. For guided tutorials, see [Python basics](01-python-basics.md), [NumPy](02-numpy.md), and [SymPy](03-symbolic-math-with-sympy.md). ::: """) return if __name__ == "__main__": app.run()

A 532 nm photon (visible) carries 2.33 eV = 225 kJ/mol: no, too weak for a C-C bond (347 kJ/mol)
 e_q%20%3D%20const.h%20*%20const.c%20%2F%20(lam_q.value%20*%201e-9)%0Akjmol_q%20%3D%20e_q%20*%20const.N_A%20%2F%201000%0Aregion_q%20%3D%20%22ultraviolet%22%20if%20lam_q.value%20%3C%20380%20else%20(%22visible%22%20if%20lam_q.value%20%3C%3D%20750%20else%20%22infrared%22)%0Averdict_q%20%3D%20%22YES%2C%20breaks%20a%20C-C%20bond%20(347%20kJ%2Fmol)%22%20if%20kjmol_q%20%3E%20347%20else%20%22no%2C%20too%20weak%20for%20a%20C-C%20bond%20(347%20kJ%2Fmol)%22%0Amo.vstack(%5B%0A%20%20%20%20mo.md(f%22A%20**%7Blam_q.value%7D%20nm**%20photon%20(%7Bregion_q%7D)%20carries%20**%7Be_q%2Fconst.e%3A.2f%7D%20eV**%20%22%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20f%22%3D%20**%7Bkjmol_q%3A.0f%7D%20kJ%2Fmol**%3A%20**%7Bverdict_q%7D**%22)%2C%0A%20%20%20%20mo.Html(f%22%3Cdiv%20style%3D'width%3A120px%3Bheight%3A14px%3Bborder-radius%3A7px%3Bbackground%3A%7Bto_hex(wavelength_to_rgb(lam_q.value))%7D'%3E%3C%2Fdiv%3E%22)%2C%0A%5D)

to_joules%20%3D%20%7B%0A%20%20%20%20%22J%22%3A%201.0%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22eV%22%3A%201.602176634e-19%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22cm%5E-1%22%3A%201.986445857e-23%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22kJ%2Fmol%22%3A%201000%20%2F%206.02214076e23%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22hartree%22%3A%204.3597447222e-18%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22Hz%22%3A%206.62607015e-34%2C%0A%7D%0Aamount_c%20%3D%20mo.ui.number(value%3D1.0%2C%20step%3D0.1%2C%20label%3D%22value%22)%0Aunit_c%20%3D%20mo.ui.dropdown(options%3Dlist(to_joules)%2C%20value%3D%22eV%22%2C%20label%3D%22from%20unit%22)%0Amo.hstack(%5Bamount_c%2C%20unit_c%5D%2C%20justify%3D%22start%22%2C%20gap%3D1.5)

; results render as typeset math. Each pad is editable:

model.html: ||[ - 2 u e^{- u^{2}} \sin{\left(u \right)} + e^{- u^{2}} \cos{\left(u \right)} ||]
||[ 2 ||]

<marimo-island
    data-app-id="jb-80ea6b018503"
    data-cell-idx="12"
    data-reactive="true"
>
    <marimo-cell-output>
    <marimo-json-output data-json-data='["text/html:\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cmarimo-tex class=\"arithmatex\"\u003e||[\n1\n||]\u003c/marimo-tex\u003e\u003c/span\u003e","text/html:\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cmarimo-tex class=\"arithmatex\"\u003e||[\n2\n||]\u003c/marimo-tex\u003e\u003c/span\u003e","text/html:\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cmarimo-tex class=\"arithmatex\"\u003e||[\n3\n||]\u003c/marimo-tex\u003e\u003c/span\u003e"]' data-value-types='"python"'></marimo-json-output>

children.value: Pick quantum numbers; the menus only ever offer valid combinations. Drag to rotate the orbital (blue and red are the wavefunction’s positive and negative lobes):

model.html: def%20radial_c(r%2C%20n%3D1%2C%20l%3D0)%3A%0A%20%20%20%20pre%20%3D%20np.sqrt(((2%20%2F%20n)%20**%203%20*%20factorial(n%20-%20l%20-%201))%20%2F%20(2%20*%20n%20*%20factorial(n%20%2B%20l)))%0A%20%20%20%20p%20%3D%202%20*%20r%20%2F%20n%0A%20%20%20%20return%20pre%20*%20np.exp(-p%20%2F%202)%20*%20p**l%20*%20genlaguerre(n%20-%20l%20-%201%2C%202%20*%20l%20%2B%201)(p)

    <marimo-cell-code hidden>d_val%20%3D%20d_sep.value%0Ax_o%20%3D%20np.linspace(-8%2C%208%2C%20800)%0Apsi_A%20%3D%20np.exp(-np.abs(x_o%20%2B%20d_val%20%2F%202))%20%2F%20np.sqrt(np.pi)%0Apsi_B%20%3D%20np.exp(-np.abs(x_o%20-%20d_val%20%2F%202))%20%2F%20np.sqrt(np.pi)%0AS_curve_d%20%3D%20np.linspace(0.01%2C%208%2C%20200)%0AS_curve%20%3D%20np.exp(-S_curve_d)%20*%20(1%20%2B%20S_curve_d%20%2B%20S_curve_d**2%20%2F%203)%0AS_now%20%3D%20np.exp(-d_val)%20*%20(1%20%2B%20d_val%20%2B%20d_val**2%20%2F%203)%0A%0Afig_o%2C%20(ax_oa%2C%20ax_ob)%20%3D%20plt.subplots(1%2C%202%2C%20figsize%3D(9.5%2C%203.6))%0Aax_oa.plot(x_o%2C%20psi_A%2C%20lw%3D1.8%2C%20color%3D%22steelblue%22%2C%20label%3Dr%22%24%5Cpsi_A%24%20(1s)%22)%0Aax_oa.plot(x_o%2C%20psi_B%2C%20lw%3D1.8%2C%20color%3D%22seagreen%22%2C%20label%3Dr%22%24%5Cpsi_B%24%20(1s)%22)%0Aax_oa.fill_between(x_o%2C%20psi_A%20*%20psi_B%20*%206%2C%20color%3D%22crimson%22%2C%20alpha%3D0.35%2C%20label%3Dr%22%24%5Cpsi_A%20%5Cpsi_B%24%20(scaled)%22)%0Aax_oa.axvline(-d_val%20%2F%202%2C%20color%3D%220.6%22%2C%20ls%3D%22%3A%22%2C%20lw%3D1)%0Aax_oa.axvline(d_val%20%2F%202%2C%20color%3D%220.6%22%2C%20ls%3D%22%3A%22%2C%20lw%3D1)%0Aax_oa.set_xlabel(%22x%20(Bohr)%22)%0Aax_oa.legend(fontsize%3D8%2C%20frameon%3DFalse)%0Aax_oa.set_title(%22two%201s%20orbitals%20(cut%20along%20the%20bond%20axis)%22%2C%20fontsize%3D10)%0A%0Aax_ob.plot(S_curve_d%2C%20S_curve%2C%20lw%3D2%2C%20color%3D%220.4%22)%0Aax_ob.plot(d_val%2C%20S_now%2C%20%22o%22%2C%20ms%3D9%2C%20color%3D%22crimson%22)%0Aax_ob.set_xlabel(%22separation%20d%20(Bohr)%22)%0Aax_ob.set_ylabel(%22overlap%20S(d)%22)%0Aax_ob.set_title(f%22S(%7Bd_val%3A.2f%7D)%20%3D%20%7BS_now%3A.3f%7D%22%2C%20fontsize%3D10)%0Aax_ob.set_ylim(0%2C%201.02)%0Afig_o.tight_layout()%0Afig_o</marimo-cell-code>
</marimo-island>
